title: Vacuum Leak Energy Loss Calculator
description: Industrial-grade calculator to estimate energy loss, cost, and carbon emissions from vacuum leaks in compressed air systems, based on ISO 1217, ISO 8573-1, and Lean Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Equivalent Leak Diameter (mm)
inputs.businessContext: Estimated diameter of the leak orifice, typically measured via ultrasonic leak detector or flow test.

inputs.label: System Pressure (bar gauge)
inputs.businessContext: Operating pressure of the compressed air system at the point of leak.

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Total annual hours the vacuum/compressed air system is in operation.

inputs.label: Electricity Cost ($/kWh)
inputs.businessContext: Average cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour, including demand charges if applicable.

inputs.label: Compressor Specific Power (kW per 100 cfm)
inputs.businessContext: Power consumption of the compressor per 100 cfm of free air delivered. Typical range 16-22 kW/100 cfm for rotary screw compressors.

inputs.label: Ambient Temperature (°C)
inputs.businessContext: Average ambient temperature at compressor intake, affects air density and mass flow.

inputs.label: Leak Type (Orifice Shape Factor)
inputs.businessContext: Shape factor affects discharge coefficient. Round is typical for drilled holes; sharp for cracks; long for pipes.

inputs.label: Include Carbon Cost Calculation
inputs.businessContext: If enabled, calculates CO2 emissions and associated cost based on grid emission factor.

inputs.label: Grid Emission Factor (kg CO2/kWh)
inputs.businessContext: Average CO2 emissions per kWh of electricity consumed. Varies by region (e.g., US average ~0.4, China ~0.6).

rules.message: Leak diameter must be greater than 0 mm.
System pressure must be between 1 and 15 bar gauge.
Operating hours must be between 1000 and 8760 hours per year.
Electricity cost must be greater than 0.
Specific power must be between 10 and 30 kW/100 cfm.
Ambient temperature must be between -10°C and 50°C.

rules.condition: include_carbon_cost == false OR (include_carbon_cost == true AND emission_factor_kg_co2_per_kwh > 0)
rules.message: Emission factor must be positive if carbon cost is included.

thresholds.message: Annual energy loss exceeds 50,000 kWh. Immediate leak repair recommended.
Annual cost exceeds $10,000. Urgent action required.
Annual CO2 emissions exceed 20,000 kg. Consider sustainability initiatives.

formulas.label: Discharge Coefficient (Cd)
formulas.description: Selects the discharge coefficient based on leak type.
formulas.expression: CASE leak_type WHEN 'round' THEN 0.62 WHEN 'sharp' THEN 0.85 WHEN 'long' THEN 0.50 END

formulas.label: Orifice Area (m²)
formulas.description: Cross-sectional area of the leak orifice.

formulas.label: Absolute Pressure (Pa)
formulas.description: Absolute pressure at the leak (gauge + atmospheric).

formulas.label: Air Density (kg/m³)
formulas.description: Density of air at ambient temperature and absolute pressure (ideal gas law).

formulas.label: Mass Flow Rate (kg/s)
formulas.description: Mass flow of air through the leak using orifice flow equation (choked flow assumed).

formulas.label: Volumetric Flow Rate (cfm)
formulas.description: Volumetric flow rate at standard conditions (1 atm, 20°C) converted to cfm.

formulas.label: Power Loss (kW)
formulas.description: Power required to compress the leaked air, based on specific power.

formulas.label: Annual Energy Loss (kWh)
formulas.description: Total energy lost per year due to the leak.

formulas.label: Annual Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Total cost of energy lost per year.

formulas.label: Annual CO2 Emissions (kg)
formulas.description: CO2 emissions associated with the energy loss (if enabled).
formulas.expression: CASE WHEN include_carbon_cost = true THEN annual_energy_loss_kwh * emission_factor_kg_co2_per_kwh ELSE 0 END

formulas.label: Annual Carbon Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Monetary cost of CO2 emissions at a social cost of carbon (assumed $50/tonne).

formulas.label: Total Annual Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Sum of energy cost and carbon cost.

primary.label: Total Annual Cost (USD)
primary.description: Total annual cost including energy and carbon (if enabled).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Leak Diameter Sensitivity
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Small increases in leak diameter cause exponential increase in loss. A 1 mm increase can double the cost.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Pressure Sensitivity
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Higher system pressure increases mass flow proportionally. Reducing pressure by 1 bar can reduce loss by ~14%.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Operating Hours Sensitivity
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Reducing operating hours (e.g., shutdowns) directly reduces annual loss.

suggestedActions.description: Perform ultrasonic leak detection survey to locate and tag all leaks. Target: repair leaks > 2 mm within 1 week.
Implement a leak management program with quarterly audits and repair KPIs (e.g., < 5% leakage rate).
Consider installing variable speed drive (VSD) compressors to match supply with demand and reduce baseline leakage impact.
Evaluate system pressure reduction: lowering pressure by 1 bar can save 7-10% in energy costs.
Train maintenance staff on proper fitting, hose, and connection inspection to prevent future leaks.
